title: [FIRTTL] Conversion ordering issue of Firtool when lowering to hw / sv
body:
Hello, while I was converting mlirs that were translated from firttl to hw or sv via arguments `--ir-sv` `--ir-hw`, I found some commonal problems when want to continue translating MLIR into LLVM or LLHD dialects. Here are some examples:
1. Use an offical test: firtool.fir `--ir-sv` to firtool-pure.mlir
Look at one of modules in it:

```
  hw.module @test_mod(%clock: i1, %a: i1, %b: i2, %vec_0: i1, %vec_1: i1, %vec_2: i1) -> (c: i1, out_implicitTrunc: i1, out_prettifyExample: i1, out_multibitMux: i1) {
    %c0_i10 = hw.constant 0 : i10
    %c0_i3 = hw.constant 0 : i3
    %cat.d = hw.instance "cat" @Cat(a: %b: i2, b: %b: i2, c: %b: i2) -> (d: i6)
    %implicitTrunc.out1, %implicitTrunc.out2 = hw.instance "implicitTrunc" @ImplicitTrunc(inp_1: %a: i1, inp_2: %0: i5) -> (out1: i3, out2: i3)
    %0 = comb.extract %cat.d from 0 : (i6) -> i5
    %prettifyExample.out1, %prettifyExample.out2 = hw.instance "prettifyExample" @PrettifyExample(inp_1: %0: i5, inp_2: %0: i5, inp_3: %0: i5) -> (out1: i10, out2: i10)
    %flipFlop.a_q = hw.instance "flipFlop" @FlipFlop(clock: %clock: i1, a_d: %a: i1) -> (a_q: i1)
    %multibitMux.b = hw.instance "multibitMux" @MultibitMux(a_0: %vec_0: i1, a_1: %vec_1: i1, a_2: %vec_2: i1, sel: %b: i2) -> (b: i1)
    %1 = comb.icmp bin ne %implicitTrunc.out1, %c0_i3 : i3
    %2 = comb.icmp bin ne %implicitTrunc.out2, %c0_i3 : i3
    %3 = comb.or bin %1, %2 : i1
    %4 = comb.icmp bin ne %prettifyExample.out1, %c0_i10 : i10
    %5 = comb.icmp bin ne %prettifyExample.out2, %c0_i10 : i10
    %6 = comb.or bin %4, %5 : i1
    hw.output %flipFlop.a_q, %3, %6, %multibitMux.b : i1, i1, i1, i1
  }
```

And when try to translate to llvm or llhd:
(I implement a customized way via HWToFunc to translate hw structural operations to LLVM)
```(I implement a customized way via HWToFunc to translate hw structural operations via HWToFunc)
❯ circt-opt firtool-pure.mlir --convert-hw-to-llvm
firtool-pure.mlir:42:48: error: operand #1 does not dominate this use
    %implicitTrunc.out1, %implicitTrunc.out2 = hw.instance "implicitTrunc" @ImplicitTrunc(inp_1: %a: i1, inp_2: %0: i5) -> (out1: i3, out2: i3)
                                               ^
firtool-pure.mlir:42:48: note: see current operation: %3 = "llvm.call"(%arg1, %6) {callee = @ImplicitTrunc, fastmathFlags = #llvm.fastmath<none>} : (i1, i5) -> !llvm.struct<(i3, i3)>
firtool-pure.mlir:43:10: note: operand defined here (op in the same block)
    %0 = comb.extract %cat.d from 0 : (i6) -> i5
         ^
```
```
❯ circt-opt firtool-pure.mlir --convert-hw-to-llhd
firtool-pure.mlir:43:10: error: operand #1 does not dominate this use
    %0 = comb.extract %cat.d from 0 : (i6) -> i5
         ^
firtool-pure.mlir:43:10: note: see current operation: "llhd.drv"(%6, %14, %7) : (!llhd.sig<i5>, i5, !llhd.time) -> ()
firtool-pure.mlir:43:10: note: operand defined here (op in the same block)
```
We could find that the location of instruction "%0 = comb.extract ..." seems to be problematic, and cause the conversion failure of previous instance operation for **"doesnot dominate this use"**. These problems occur when InstanceOp uses the result of a combOp with the wrong location as an argument.

Is this a bug? Looking forware to further answers and replies.
